note: Pure measurement task. Establishes an Edax level-12 baseline for future gates; no adoption/verdict is rendered.
harnessNote: vs_edax.py's --levels argument already accepts an arbitrary comma-separated int list (parsed via int(x), passed directly as `-l <level>` to Edax); no harness modification was needed to run level 12.

commits: f693d05 (T170: production wiring, node-budget gate updated to v5 reference)
910235b (T172: MPC recalibration attempt, Gate 3 failed, withdrawn per pre-registration)
engineChangesSinceT169.reuseNotApplicableReason: This is a fresh measurement at a different Edax level (12) than any prior gate (all prior gates used level 10); no prior level-12 data exists to reuse regardless of engine version.

protocol.sourceOfTruth: identical to T158d/T162/T166/T169 except Edax level (10 -> 12)

execution.actualVsEstimate: completed in ~14.3 minutes, well under the 30-60 minute estimate

results.conclusion: lv12 is a measurably harder benchmark for v6; result serves as the new gauge baseline for future lv12 gates. No verdict is rendered.

phaseBandTrend.definition: For each game, the discDiff telemetry (engine-perspective disc-count evaluation, from either the budgeted or exact-solve phase) of the LAST engine move within each phase is taken, where phase classification follows vs_edax.py's classify_phase() (opening: truePly 1-20, midgame: 21-40, endgame: 41+). Mean/median computed over all 60 games per phase. The endgame-phase value converges to the final game margin.

lv10Reference.source: recomputed from T169's t169-d1-vs-edax-results-full.json (v6 at lv10)

phaseBandTrend.interpretation: At lv10, the deficit grows slowly through opening/midgame and only becomes pronounced in the endgame (-0.53 -> -2.23). At lv12, the deficit is already about 2.4x larger by the end of the opening (-0.79 vs -0.33), roughly 4x larger by the end of midgame (-2.16 vs -0.53), and about 2.7x larger by the endgame (-6.07 vs -2.23). This suggests Edax's greater strength at lv12 asserts itself progressively from an early stage, not only in the endgame solve -- the midgame heuristic-evaluation phase shows the largest relative widening. No causal analysis was performed (observation only, per task scope).

budgetedToExactDeviation.definitionNote: Per T169 verifier follow-up request, the deviation metric is defined explicitly: for each game, take the LAST engine move's discDiff telemetry during the budgeted phase (root empties ~21-22, time/node-budgeted search) and the FIRST engine move's discDiff telemetry during the unlimited-exact phase (root empties <=20, full solve). The absolute difference between these two values is the 'deviation' for that game. Identical definition to T158d/T162/T166/T169.
budgetedToExactDeviation.exactFallbackDefinition: Counts ONLY the single transition move (last budgeted move before empties<=20). Identical to T158d/T162/T166/T169.
budgetedToExactDeviation.interpretation: lv12's deviation (mean 4.68) is somewhat larger than lv10's (mean 4.31), but not qualitatively different. Sign-flip counts are identical (4/60 each), all originating from small near-zero budgeted values; no catastrophic reversal on either level.

verdict: none (pure measurement task, no adoption/gate decision)
outOfScope: root-cause analysis of the midgame/endgame widening
